sections.content: Search and Rescue operations are coordinated through a hierarchical structure. The Maritime Rescue Coordination Centre (MRCC) receives distress alerts, assesses the situation, and dispatches Search and Rescue Units (SRUs). When multiple units are on scene, an On-Scene Coordinator (OSC) is designated to manage local operations.

rows: Receives alerts, plans and coordinates the overall SAR operation
Coordinates all SAR units at the scene, usually a vessel or aircraft
Vessels, aircraft, or teams directly conducting the search and rescue
Provides information, activates beacons, follows instructions from MRCC/OSC

sections.content: Communication during SAR operations typically uses Channel 16 for initial contact and a designated on-scene working channel. The OSC assigns search patterns (parallel track, expanding square, sector search) based on the situation and available units.

sections.prompt: What is the role of the On-Scene Coordinator?

options.text: To receive distress alerts from satellites
To coordinate SAR units at the scene of the emergency
To issue weather warnings during the search
To investigate the cause of the distress

sections.explanation: The On-Scene Coordinator (OSC) is designated by the MRCC to coordinate all SAR units at the scene of the emergency, assign search patterns, and manage on-scene communications.
